Class ConcreteLexer

java.lang.Object
dk.tij.registermaschine.core.compilation.ConcreteLexer
All Implemented Interfaces:
dk.tij.registermaschine.api.compilation.ILexer
public final class ConcreteLexer extends Object implements dk.tij.registermaschine.api.compilation.ILexer
Concrete implementation of ILexer for the Registermaschine.

This lexer transforms a source code string into a list of ITokens suitable for parsing. It handles instructions, registers, numbers, labels, comments, and special address/immediate syntax. It also performs basic validation and reports lexical errors for malformed tokens.

Supported token types:

  • TokenType.INSTRUCTION – recognised mnemonics from the IInstructionSet
  • TokenType.REGISTER – registers prefixed with 'r' (e.g., r0, r1)
  • TokenType.NUMBER – immediate values prefixed with '#' and optionally hexadecimal
  • TokenType.LABEL – symbolic addresses or labels
  • TokenType.LABEL_DEF – label definitions ending with ':'
  • TokenType.COMMA – comma separators between operands
  • TokenType.EOL – end-of-line markers
  • TokenType.EOF – end-of-file marker
  • TokenType.COMMENT – comments starting with ';'
  • TokenType.UNKNOWN – unrecognised characters
  • TokenType.ERROR – malformed tokens (e.g., invalid immediate/address)

Lexer features:

  • Normalizes line endings to '\n'
  • Tracks line and column positions for error reporting
  • Supports hexadecimal addresses and immediates
  • Handles symbolic labels based on CoreConfig.ALLOW_LABELS
  • Validates register names using CoreConfig.TOKEN_REGEX
Since:
1.0.0
Author:
TiJ

    • tokenize

      public List<dk.tij.registermaschine.api.compilation.lexing.IToken> tokenize(String sourceCode, dk.tij.registermaschine.api.instructions.IInstructionSet instructions)
      Tokenises the given source code using the provided instruction set.
      Specified by:
      tokenize in interface dk.tij.registermaschine.api.compilation.ILexer
      Parameters:
      sourceCode - the source code string to tokenise
      instructions - the instruction set to recognise instruction tokens
      Returns:
      a list of ITokens representing the lexed source code
